title: Borderlands 2

notes:
Native version can't play co-op with people on Windows right now (hopefully this will get resolved), so I decided to give the Windows version a shot. I put some launch arguments and renamed the launcher as previous contributors suggested, and the game ran almost flawlessly -- save for some minor graphical glitches such as, but not limited to, the water lacking any form of texture. It also crashed one of the times that I tried to join a friend, but following that I didn't have any issues doing so. 

Performance was slightly worse than Windows (though very much playable), and since BL2 only runs using DX9, it can't use DXVK unfortunately. I tried forcing D3D11 by editing WillowEngine.ini, but no dice. Seems to have made no difference whatsoever. Attempting to enable OpenGL in the same file made the game crash on startup.
title: Borderlands 2

notes: It looks like during my install, it didn't download d3d11 or direct x.  So what I did was go into a working steam game, copy those two folders into my Borderlands game and it works.  It plays just as well as on Windows.  I got lucky but I'll take it.  Now to try it on Killing Floor 2 which isn't working on my machine either.

title: Borderlands 2

notes:
At first, renaming Borderlands2.exe to Launcher.exe didn't work. Using any of the tweaks didn't work either.

What worked for me was disabling the DLCs, reinstalling the Linux client, running the game using the Linux client, enabling Proton, renaming Borderlands2.exe to Launcher.exe, then reinstalling the DLCs. After that, the game worked but with reduced performance (since I haven't set up D9VK yet)
title: Borderlands 2

notes:
the game launches after symlinking the game executable to the launcher with `ln -sf $HOME/steam/steam/steamapps/common/Borderlands\ 2/Binaries/Win32/Borderlands2.exe $HOME/steam/steam/steamapps/common/Borderlands\ 2/Binaries/Win32/Launcher.exe`

after that the game works mostly fine, however there seems to be a bug where you can pick up ammo even if you have 100% you can hold of that ammo, and the ammo you pick up gets deleted.
title: Borderlands 2

notes: Native version would not launch for me, so I had to try this instead. The launcher seems to break, however after running these commands in the "~/.steam/Steam/steamapps/Borderlands 2/Binaries/Win32" directory the game runs perfectly: `rm Launcher.exe` and `ln -sf Borderlands2.exe Launcher.exe`
title: Borderlands 2

notes:
Neither executable renaming nor symlinking necessary. Installed protonfixes, works like a charm.

Cloud-synced saves seem to be separate from native, but this is easily remedied by copying between "~/.local/share/aspyr-media/Borderlands 2/" and the corresponding directory under the proton prefix.

With the window mode set to Fullscreen, the game locks up when losing focus, but Windowed Fullscreen seems to work fine.

The main menu seems to ignore the mouse cursor initially, though focus changes seem to fix this... not sure. Possibly game-controller related?

Performance is acceptable, though not stellar, with default configuration. I may attempt d9vk to see if it's better.
title: Borderlands 2

notes: Tried the windows version because of UHD textures, and it runs way better than the native version, but only if I use D9VK (dropped the d3d9.dll in the binaries folder and loaded it with "WINEDLLOVERRIDES="d3d9=n" DXVK_HUD=full %command%"). However, the game crashed when I tried to travel to another area.

title: Borderlands 2

notes:
Game started after renaming Borderlands2.exe to Launcher.exe (renamed Launcher.exe to Launcher.exe.old).  I used the launch options "-nomoviestartup -NoLauncher" and game runs with and without d9vk (v0.12) fine. 

The only issue I had would be some introductionary cutscenes in sanctuary not playing correctly (The HUD would disappear and you couldn't move while the cutscene was supposed to be playing, but after it finished for the other players the game continued normally), else no other problems were encountered.

Played the game mostly in multiplayer with Windows users and a short time in singleplayer.
title: Borderlands 2

notes:
Runs fine after installing d9vk (by dropping the d3d9.dll file next to the game executable (https://github.com/Joshua-Ashton/d9vk/releases)), renaming "Launcher.exe" to something else and "Borderlands2.exe" to "Launcher.exe" and setting as launch option "WINEDLLOVERRIDES="d3d9=n" %command%".
After that, I had no problem at all, performance is way better with D9VK than without.

Should your game freeze or crash more or less frequently, try setting Texture Quality to Medium (https://github.com/Joshua-Ashton/d9vk/issues/170).
title: Borderlands 2

notes: Without D9VK, the game runs at about 40-50 FPS in combat or otherwise crowded areas, and 60 elsewhere, but almost randomly freezes up, slowing the whole system down. With D9VK, performance is much better, almost constant 60 FPS, however it reliably freezes after fast traveling to intensive areas, occasionally with a "ran out of video memory" error. Multiplayer with a friend slowed to 10-15 FPS in combat, however that is not thoroughly tested.
title: Borderlands 2

notes: I'm playing this game about 5 years (?) now and after this DLC update... Well I was forced to upgrade to the Windows version. Even tho game works great when was native... Game unplayable with both wined3d and d9vk, on wined3d you will get 10-30 fps, on d9vk you will run out of VRAM. First I through that game running out 32bit addressing space, but according to desktop starting to freeze and compositor crash it WAS out of VRAM. So we have some kind of memory leak on d9vk. Nothing will help, unfortunately, I tried to disable esync but no luck.

title: Borderlands 2

notes: Aside from the usual Launcher tweaks to get the game to run it performs flawlessly at 2160p with UHD texture pack so long as you drop the texture quality to medium. The game easily runs out of memory and crashes with textures maxed out. Using D9VK I get a full 60fps in most areas and wide open spaces. You may need to turn down some eyecandy settings like ambient occlusion and depth of field during heavier battles. Adaptive sync aka Freesync is working if your monitor is supported and its enabled. You can also enable CPU based physx eye-candy by manually editing the WillowEngine.ini config file which doesn't impact performance too much. It has been 7 months since the native version was out of sync with the Windows release, this is your only way to obtain crossplay. The fact that the game has a native release should be deemphasized in light of impressive performance via Proton/D9VK and the broken crossplay of the native release.
title: Borderlands 2

title: Borderlands 2

notes: Native version does not work for MP (https://bit.ly/2lb7kxj) there was a windows update, but the developer for Linux (Aspyr) is working on a fix. In the mean time use Proton. I had to compeltely uninstall the linux version, and to force it to download the windows one I had to open the game properties in your steam library, and tick "force the use of steam play "4.11-5". Set launch options to "PROTON_USE_D9VK=1 %command%". Run the game first (to generate the proton files) then follow this guide - https://bit.ly/2mayvZx (Note I had to change the directory because my steam is not in .steam, its on a different steam library folder, instead it is located at "/LibraryLocation/SteamLibrary/steamapps/compatdata". Also note any tweaking of the engine.ini stops the game working, so only tweak GFX in game. Pretty solid performance otherwise.
title: Borderlands 2

notes: Mesa 19.3 fixed the stuttering issues I had with 19.1. The game now runs flawlessly after the following tweaks: [ 1 ] Install the Mesa ACO Driver (mesa-aco-git and lib32-mesa-aco-git from the AUR) [ 2 ] Enable D9VK (Set the Steam launch options to "PROTON_USE_D9VK=1 %command%")
title: Borderlands 2

notes: I added PROTON_USE_D9VK=1 %command% in Steam launch options. I also went to /steamapps/common/Borderlands 2/Binaries/Win32/ and renamed Borderlands2.exe to Launcher.exe by renaming the existing to Launcher.old. This was necessary to play multiplayer coop with my friend. When launching natively, it would say incompatible game version when trying to play with a friend. After that, the game ran smoothly.
title: Borderlands 2

notes: Worked fine on stock Fedora 31 after doing the Launcher.exe tweak and applying D9VK, but performance was lacking. Runs extremely well after installing mesa-git, llvm-git and the ACO driver.
title: Borderlands 2

notes: Runs great, using Valve's ACO Mesa driver fixes the stuttering and greatly improves performance but causes some weird error where the game will crash and say it's out of video memory. Though this only happens with the UHD texture DLC and the ACO driver.  Install the driver by either adding the PPA or just using your favourite AUR package manager such as Pamac or Yay to install it (it'll automatically remove any existing Mesa installation). I'll have to look into why it runs out of vram, but I imagine this will be fixed before ACO is rolled into Mesa 19.3
title: Borderlands 2

title: Borderlands 2

notes:
When Borderlands 2 got the new DLC tie-in for Borderlands 3, only the Windows version was updated. Therefore, in the "Borderlands 2 - Properties" dialogue, check "Force the use of a specific Steam Play compatibility tool" to download the Windows version instead of running native.

The launcher doesn't run, so you'll want to skip it by swapping the Launcher.exe with Borderlands2.exe or making a symlink or whatever other method you prefer.

D9VK is required to avoid rendering glitches. If you experience poor performance or out of memory crashes, disable esync and force large address aware, respectively. I also use gamemoderun from https://github.com/FeralInteractive/gamemode for maximum performance, but this isn't required.

The final launch options with all of these tweaks combined is "PROTON_USE_D9VK=1 PROTON_FORCE_LARGE_ADDRESS_AWARE=1 PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 gamemoderun %command%"
title: Borderlands 2

notes: Default installation will not work unless you rename Launcher.exe with Borderlands2.exe which is located at /steamapps/common/Borderlands 2/Binaries/Win32/. The game runs slightly better by applying D9VK tweak and also adding the d3d9.dll file into the game directory. Have not experience any crashes or errors as of now.
